Fixing 'Blocked request' When Exposing a Nuxt 3 Dev Server Through ngrok

A quick note on fixing "Blocked request. This host is not allowed" when exposing a Nuxt 3 dev server through ngrok — including why Nuxt points you at a `vite.config.js` that doesn't exist, where to actually put `server.allowedHosts` in `nuxt.config.ts`, how to use the leading-dot wildcard to cover all ngrok subdomains, and why setting `allowedHosts: true` is a bad idea.
How to Dockerize a Vite + Vue Project
Want to ship a Vite + Vue app as a Docker image? A line-by-line multi-stage Dockerfile walkthrough — from the build stage to the Nginx-served runtime.
Monorepo? Another Way to Manage Projects — Building a Vue3 Monorepo With Vite + pnpm

What is a Monorepo? How is it different from a regular project setup? I had a need for one recently, so I wrote this up. The post walks through Monolith vs Multi vs Monorepo, plus a hands-on Vue3 Monorepo build using Vite + pnpm.
Using Proxy in Vite to Fix CORS Issues
Frontend devs hit CORS constantly. Walkthrough of Vite's built-in proxy — string form, options form, and how to route dev requests through to dodge CORS.
How to Import Images in Vue (Vue CLI and Vite)
A quick note on how to import images properly in Vue CLI or Vite. It's a common question and easy to get wrong, so I figured I'd write it down.
Setting the Correct Build Path for Vue CLI and Vite

This post covers how to properly set the build path for Vue CLI and Vite. It actually matters a lot — if you don't set it right, both your local dev and post-build paths will break. So here's how to do it correctly.
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now? - Vite: Why It's So Fast & Deploying to GitHub Pages - (23)
You'll Learn React Eventually, So Why Not Now?

In the last chapter we got our Vite + React + Tailwind CSS environment ready, so now it's time to move everything over from CRA to Vite. After that, we'll take a quick look at why Vite is so fast under the hood, and walk through two different ways to deploy the project to GitHub Pages.
React, You'll Learn It Eventually — Why Not Now? - Setting Up React with Vite - (22)
React, You'll Learn It Eventually — Why Not Now?

This is where we kick off a whole new chapter: Vite. A lot of you have probably already used Vite on a project, and if you haven't, no worries — I'll walk you through why Vite is so nice to work with, and how to actually use it. We'll build a Vite-for-React project from scratch, poke at its folder structure, and wire up Tailwind CSS along the way.
How to Add ESLint to a Vue Vite Project
This post walks through adding ESLint to a Vue Vite project, using the Airbnb or Standard style guide.
Vite Deployed to GitHub Pages but _plugin-vue_export-helper Returns 404

Vite builds and ships to GitHub Pages fine — but once you load the page, the dynamically imported _plugin-vue_export-helper module 404s and the page goes blank. This post walks through the actual cause (Rollup's filename sanitization producing a name GitHub Pages doesn't serve) and the vite.config.js tweak that fixes it.
